name: Contacts
description: CRM people mirrored into Personize with AI-filled lifecycle, scoring, personalization, workspace, and sequence properties.

properties.description: Primary email address from the connected CRM.
Contact first name.
Contact last name.
Primary phone number from the connected CRM.
Current role or title, extracted or normalized from CRM, signatures, LinkedIn, transcripts, or forms.
Normalized seniority derived from job title and context.
Normalized business function or department.

Canonical company domain used to link the contact to a company. Resolved from the CRM contact->company association (HubSpot: companies; Salesforce: the Contact->Account lookup — Leads have no Account and fall back to the Company text field), keyed to the synced company's domain.
CRM where this record originated.
Native CRM object represented by this Personize contact.
Native CRM record identifier for writeback.
0-100 lead or stakeholder score based on ICP fit, engagement, lifecycle stage, and recent signals.
One-sentence explanation for the latest AI score.
Inferred buying stage from CRM activity, conversations, meetings, and signals.
Recommended next action for the agent or human owner.
Most recent meaningful signal affecting this contact.
Current sentiment toward us, inferred from replies, calls, and engagement patterns.
Preferred tone — direct, consultative, technical, casual. Inferred from how this contact writes.
Specific challenges, frustrations, or needs mentioned or inferred from conversations and activity.
Professional interests, topics this contact engages with.

Current contact-state summary — enrichment status, ICP score, sequence step, last engagement, recommended next action. Rewritten each cycle by whichever agent has the latest understanding. The 'start here' for anyone engaging with this contact.

Chronological timeline of everything that happened. Each entry: { author, type (observation|action|change|milestone|enrichment|signal|outreach|engagement|system), summary, details, timestamp }. Append-only — how agents and humans see what others did.
Knowledge and observations from any contributor. Each entry: { author, content, category (observation|analysis|idea|reference|question|enrichment|signal|reply-analysis), timestamp }. Append-only.

Choices made about this contact with full reasoning — the explainability layer. Each entry: { question, decision, reasoning, alternatives, confidence (low|medium|high), autonomous (boolean), approvedBy, timestamp }. Append-only. If a decision is reversed, add a new entry explaining why.
Latest sales-playbook generation status
Composed rep-facing sales playbook
Sales playbook section: account snapshot
Sales playbook section: why now
Sales playbook section: talk track
Sales playbook section: landmines
Sales playbook section: next step
Latest landing-zone generation status
Landing zone: hero headline. Example zone property; additional zones are provisioned the same way per campaign: add a zone_<name> longtext property to this manifest, matching this entry's shape.
Landing zone: proof paragraph. Example zone property, see zone_hero_headline.
Landing zone: call-to-action line. Example zone property, see zone_hero_headline.
Count of outreach emails sent in the current sequence. Used for cadence gating — no parsing needed.
ISO timestamp of the most recent outreach email. Used for timing-gap checks between sequence steps.
Current outreach sequence state. Updated on engagement events.
Active campaign this contact belongs to. A contact can only be in one active campaign at a time.
